title: Clandestinaria indica
intro:

Andrographis paniculata is a plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to India and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Andrographis paniculata include andrographolide, andrographis lactone, andrographisolide, and andrographin, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Andrographis paniculata include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and flu.

preparations: Clandestinaria indica is traditionally used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
